claim: The proposal protocol shall include a ProposedStakeholder kind (Kind="Stakeholder") that materializes a new Stakeholder node in the active domain's graph by appending to g.Stakeholders, refusing a duplicate id.
why: Wave 13 (the stranger's door). A newcomer who clones the repo for their own business domain is locked out at the first Conflict: check_resolver_not_a_member_owner requires a resolver who is NOT the owner of any member Requirement, i.e. at least two distinct Stakeholders must exist before any tension can be held. Yet every Requirement, Axis and Assumption already had a Proposed* door while Stakeholder did not, trapping the newcomer between R-no-hand-edit-graph (the graph is writable only through proposals) and the absence of a door. ProposedStakeholder is that door: a struct in internal/proposal/types.go (KindStakeholder="Stakeholder", fields ID/Name/Domain/Why) whose mutate method (internal/proposal/mutate.go: ProposedStakeholder.mutate) checks ontology.StakeholderIDs(g) for the exact id and, if absent, appends an ontology.Stakeholder{ID,Name,Domain} to g.Stakeholders. It models the exact ProposedAxis/ProposedAssumption pattern (append-to-slice, exact-id-uniqueness); extends R-active-loop-protocol's floor exactly as EntityType, OperatorBudget, Axis and Assumption did. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.
